LÃ©ger only published four mathematical papers but one contains possibly the first mention of what today is a well known fact about the Euclidean algorithm , see [ 1 ] :- Ãmile LÃ©ger appears to have been the first ( or second, if the work of de Lagny ... is counted ) to recognise that the worst case of the Euclidean algorithm occurs when the inputs are consecutive Fibonacci numbers .
